seed: vulnerability vs strength; light_vs_weight; mirror_broken; sweep; constellation against tide; closed_corners; thin_vs_heavy; horizon; safety_exposure | abstract variation request 209; target batch count 500
title: Eclipse of Emotions
reasoning: This artwork captures the struggle between vulnerability and strength through a tension of color and form. The palette, consisting of mourning_black, deep_blue, new_leaf, pale_yellow, and stone, evokes an airy yet heavy emotional landscape. The saturated hues of deep_blue dominate, representing strength and stability, whereas the pale_yellow suggests vulnerability and exposure. The placement showcases a broken frame layout, allowing emotions to ‘leak’ through the edges. The main interaction occurs in the center, where the brightness of pale_yellow collides with the depth of deep_blue, establishing a strong contrast that illustrates the conflict between safety and exposure. Broken pixels across the piece add intentional imperfections, suggesting the scars of emotional experiences, while maintaining an abstract quality throughout the design.

layout.name: broken_frame
layout.description: A border nearly encloses the square but has deliberate breaks where emotion leaks through.

composition.name: center_vs_edges
composition.idea: The center is protected or attacked by the surrounding pixels.

referenceExample.title: Resilient Scar
referenceExample.pattern: thin broken structure crossed by a repaired vertical path

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
